title: Banda Hits 48.2°C: Severe Heatwave Thrusts Uttar Pradesh City Into Global Spotlight - Republic World
full_text:
Banda’s extreme temperatures are not an anomaly, but a result of the combination of harsh geography and changing climate patterns.
A blistering heatwave has gripped northern India, thrusting the small city of Banda in Uttar Pradesh into the global spotlight. Registering a staggering 48.2°C, the city has officially earned a spot among the hottest places on the planet this week.
While India is no stranger to intense summer temperatures, the intensity of the current heatwave has turned Banda - a historic city in the Bundelkhand region of Uttar Pradesh - into a literal furnace, leaving Churu in Rajasthan behind to become one of the hottest cities not just in India, but globally.
According to reports, the locals have adopted a new day routine to deal with the soaring temperature by starting the day early and wrapping up work before 10AM. Even those engaged in agriculture have started using massive light in the field to work at night as the heat in the day becomes unbearable.
Local hospitals, too, have reported a sharp spike in heat-related admissions.
Why is Banda burning?
Banda’s extreme temperatures are not an anomaly, but a result of the combination of harsh geography and changing climate patterns. Experts point three main reasons behind the current situation.
The Loo Winds: The region is currently experiencing dry, scorching westerly winds blowing from the deserts in the nearby regions of Rajasthan and Sindh. These winds, popularly known as ‘loo’, act like a hot dryer blowing over the Indo-Gangetic plains.
The Bundelkhand Topography: Banda sits in a rocky, semi-arid plateau characterized by hard granite terrain and sparse forest cover. The rocky ground acts like a massive heat sink, absorbing intense solar radiation during the day and radiating it back into the atmosphere, preventing the area from cooling down even at night.
Declining Water Levels : Severe deforestation and depleted groundwater reserves in Bundelkhand mean there is virtually no moisture in the soil to provide evaporative cooling.
As the sun sets over the baked landscape of Bundelkhand, the heat stubbornly lingers in the concrete and rock, offering little respite before the furnace fires up again the next day.
Published By : Avipsha Sengupta
Published On: 21 May 2026 at 17:30 IST

title: Banda records 48.2°C as Uttar Pradesh heatwave continues - The News Mill
full_text:
Uttar Pradesh remained under severe heatwave conditions on Thursday, with Banda district recording the highest temperature in India for the third consecutive day at 48.2 degrees Celsius, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) reported. Residents in Banda sought relief from the extreme heat by consuming sherbet and water from roadside stalls.
Local resident Puneet Saxena attributed the rising temperatures to deforestation and illegal mining, stating, “It is scorching hot in Banda right now. It is a sort of ‘Agni Pariksha’ for Banda residents. I believe the reason for this rise in temperature is trees being cut down and illegal mining. Stone and sand mafia operate here.”
Another local, Syed Imran Ali, expressed similar concerns, saying, “The heat conditions are so bad that the temperature is continuously increasing. The biggest reason is illegal cutting down of trees to make roads. All the old huge trees that caused cooling due to their shadows have ceased to exist here.”
In response to rising temperatures in Lucknow, which crossed 42 degrees Celsius, Civil Hospital has established a dedicated cold room facility along with special wards for treating heat stress and heatstroke patients. Chief Medical Superintendent Devesh Chandra Pandey told ANI, “Considering the scorching heat this year, we have been directed by the government to be fully prepared. We have made a ward of 10 beds. We have set up a cold room area, which encapsulates three rooms where the AC is continuously on. We have stocked up ORS and ice packs.” He added, “We have not received a patient in a serious condition yet.”
The IMD has issued an Orange Alert for several western Uttar Pradesh districts, including Lucknow, due to the ongoing heatwave conditions.
According to meteorologist Dr Dinesh, Banda has been recording temperatures above 45 degrees Celsius for nearly a week. He explained, “Key reasons include direct sunlight due to proximity to the Tropic of Cancer, clear skies, plateau terrain with low soil moisture, drying rivers, deforestation, and mining.”
Physician Vineet Sachan reported an increase in hospital admissions for fever, dizziness, vomiting and diarrhoea linked to the heatwave. District Magistrate Amit Aseri confirmed that hospitals and ambulances had been placed on alert, and advised residents to avoid going out during peak afternoon hours.

title: Scorching Heatwave Grips India, Uttar Pradesh's Banda Records 47.6°C - prameyanews.com
full_text:
New Delhi, May 21: Various Parts of India, including Odisha, faced extreme heat on Thursday, with Banda in Uttar Pradesh emerging as the hottest spot at 47.6°C.
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) reported heatwave to severe heatwave conditions across several states, including Haryana, Delhi, Jammu & Kashmir, Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Uttar Pradesh, Punjab, and Madhya Pradesh.
Delhi continued to endure sweltering temperatures, with mercury hovering around 45°C.

title: Temperature Update Today: Heatwave alert in Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Chhattisgarh and Other States. Check Temperature - Dailyhunt
full_text:
Even as heavy rains and pre-monsoon activities intensify in southern and island regions, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued severe heatwave warnings for wide portions of North and Central India.
India continues to experience extreme and contradictory weather conditions.
The IMD reports that persistent dry north-westerly winds from the Thar Desert and surrounding areas are causing extreme heatwave conditions in Delhi, Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana, Madhya Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, Telangana, and portions of Bihar.
Temperature Update Today: No Signs of Relief
As per the IMD, maximum temperatures are in the range of 40-47°C over northwest India except the western Himalayan region, West, Central & adjoining East and North Peninsular India. The highest maximum temperature of 48.0°C was reported at Banda, Uttar Pradesh.
Punjab, Haryana, Chandigarh and Delhi are likely to witness heatwave to severe heatwave conditions on May 21 and again during May 24-26. Severe heat conditions are also expected in West Uttar Pradesh between May 21 and 26, while East Uttar Pradesh may experience similar conditions during May 21-23, extending again from May 24-26.
Madhya Pradesh is likely to see severe heat conditions in some areas between May 21-25, while Vidarbha may face severe heat from May 21-23, before easing into normal heatwave conditions till May 26.
Other states, including Chhattisgarh, Telangana, Odisha, Coastal Andhra Pradesh and Yanam are expected to experience heatwave conditions between May 21-24. Bihar may see heatwave conditions from May 21-23, while Rajasthan is expected to remain under heatwave conditions through May 26. Isolated heatwave conditions are also likely in Uttarakhand on May 21.
The IMD has additionally warned of warm night conditions in Odisha, Uttar Pradesh and Vidarbha, which could reduce nighttime relief from high temperatures.
Meanwhile, hot and humid weather is likely over Tamil Nadu, Puducherry and Karaikal, along with Gangetic West Bengal and Odisha, during the forecast period.
IMD Forecast for Delhi
With temperatures predicted to increase and no quick relief in sight, the Delhi-NCR, which is already struggling with extreme heat, may need to get ready for even harsher days.
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a "orange" advisory for Delhi, Ghaziabad, Noida, and Gurugram as the heatwave gets worse.
On Thursday, the highest temperature is predicted to stay above 45 degrees Celsius. The weather bureau reports that the colour-coded alert for excessive heat and "loo" conditions is still in effect throughout Delhi-NCR.
The capital has already recorded its sixth heatwave day of 2026, while unusually high night temperatures near 28°C provide no respite after sunset. Weather officials have warned that no significant cooling or thunderstorm activity is expected in the coming days.
Precautions to Take During a Heatwave Situation
·Drink fluids regularly: Keep sipping water every 30 minutes, even if you do not feel thirsty.
·Replenish electrolytes: Consume homemade beverages like buttermilk (chaas), lemon water, coconut water, and Oral Rehydration Solutions (ORS).
·Eat hydrating foods: Include water-rich fruits and vegetables in your diet, such as watermelons, cucumbers, and citrus fruits.
·Wear protective gear: Cover your head with an umbrella, hat, or damp cloth when stepping out.
·Clothing: Wear light-colored, loose, and lightweight cotton garments that allow your skin to breathe.

title: UP: प्रदेश में गर्मी का तांडव, लू के थपेड़ों से मची त्राहि; दस जिलों में रेड अलर्ट; जानें इसके वैज्ञानिक कारण - Amar Ujala
search_term: ("हीट एक्शन प्लान" OR "गर्मी से राहत" OR चेतावनी OR "रेड अलर्ट" OR "ऑरेंज अलर्ट" OR "येलो अलर्ट" OR एडवाइजरी OR "राहत सामग्री" OR "राहत शिविर" OR मुआवजा) Uttar Pradesh
full_text:
UP: प्रदेश में गर्मी का तांडव, लू के थपेड़ों से मची त्राहि; दस जिलों में रेड अलर्ट; जानें इसके वैज्ञानिक कारण
उत्तर प्रदेश में भीषण गर्मी और लू से जनजीवन प्रभावित हो गया है। कई जिलों में तापमान 45 डिग्री से ऊपर पहुंच गया, जबकि बांदा सबसे गर्म रहा। मौसम विभाग ने दस जिलों में रेड अलर्ट जारी किया है। पुरवाई और ट्रफ लाइन के कारण आभासी गर्मी और बढ़ गई है।
खबरें लगातार पढ़ने के लिए अमर उजाला एप डाउनलोड करें
या
वेबसाइट पर पढ़ना जारी रखने के लिए वीडियो विज्ञापन देखें
अगर आपके पास प्रीमियम मेंबरशिप है तो
उत्तर प्रदेश में बृहस्पतिवार को भीषण गर्मी का प्रकोप रहा। बुंदेलखंड और प्रदेश के दक्षिणी जिले लू की तपिश और चिलचिलाती धूप की चपेट में रहे। वहीं सात जिलों में अधिकतम तापमान 45 डिग्री के पार चला गया। ऐसे में दिन में चली पुरवाई ने आग में घी का काम किया और बढ़े हुए हीट इंडेक्स व आभासी गर्मी ने लोगों को बेचैन किया। प्रदेश में सबसे अधिक तापमान बांदा में दर्ज किया गया, जहां पारा 47.6 डिग्री सेल्सियस तक पहुंच गया।
माैसम विभाग ने शुक्रवार के लिए प्रदेश के दस जिलों के लू और प्रचंड गर्मी का रेड अलर्ट जारी किया है। वहीं 23 अन्य जिलों के लिए अतिशय तपिश की चेतावनी दी गई है। मौसम विभाग के अनुसार कई जिलों में रात का तापमान भी सामान्य से अधिक रहेगा।
आंचलिक मौसम विज्ञान केंद्र लखनऊ के वरिष्ठ वैज्ञानिक अतुल कुमार सिंह ने बताया कि नार्थ यूपी से मणिपुर से एक ट्रफ लाइन गुजर रही है। इसके चलते प्रदेश के कई जिलों में पुरवाई चल रही है। इसने आभासी गर्मी को बढ़ा दिया है। फिलहाल अगले तीन चार दिनों तक पूर्वी और दक्षिणी उत्तर प्रदेश में भीषण लू चलने की संभावना है।
इन जिलों में रेड अलर्ट
बांदा, चित्रकूट, कौशांबी, प्रयागराज, फतेहपुर, प्रतापगढ़, मिर्जापुर, वाराणसी, भदोही, जौनपुर।
सर्वाधिक तापमान वाले जिले
